title: melia azedarach var. biglandulosa
intro: Melia azedarach var. biglandulosa, also known as the chinaberry tree, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia and Australia, characterized by its distinctive purple berries and fragrant flowers. The medicinal properties of Melia azedarach var. biglandulosa include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which are attributed to its alkaloid and terpenoid compounds. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Melia azedarach var. biglandulosa include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
